title: Assertive Communication Skills Checklist
description: Track the assertive communication skills you're practicing. These skills are learned — they get easier with repetition. Focus on the ones that feel most relevant to you and build gradually.
instructions: Review each item and check off the skills you've practiced this week. It's okay to start small — even one checked item is progress. Revisit weekly to track your growing confidence with assertive communication.

items.label: I used an I-statement to express how I felt ("I feel... when... because... I'd like...")
I named a specific feeling instead of saying "I'm fine" when I wasn't
I listened to someone's perspective and reflected it back before responding with my own
I said no to something I didn't want to do, without over-explaining or apologizing excessively
I set or enforced a boundary (e.g., "I'm not okay with being yelled at")
I chose a good time for a difficult conversation (cooled down first, enough time and privacy)
I took a communicated time-out during a heated moment ("I need a break — I'll come back in 20 minutes")
I returned to a conversation after a time-out instead of avoiding it
I addressed someone's behavior without attacking their character ("That was hurtful" not "You're a jerk")
I stayed on the current issue instead of bringing up past grievances
I noticed my voice rising and deliberately lowered it
I asked about someone's intention instead of assuming it was hostile
I offered a specific, no-excuses apology for something I said or did while angry
I acknowledged how my anger affected someone else
I made a specific commitment to do something differently next time (not just "I'll try harder")
I practiced being assertive in a low-stakes situation (returning food, declining an invitation, stating a preference)

disclaimer: This checklist tracks communication skills for educational purposes. It does not replace professional support. If anger involves physical violence, threats, or harm to others, please seek professional help. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
